items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).

items.q: What is the difference between strength and power?
items.correct: Strength is maximum force; power is force times speed of movement.
items.random_wrong: Strength is for the upper body, power for the lower.
items.why: Students conflate strength and power. A strong person can lift heavy slowly; a powerful athlete generates force QUICKLY (sprinter, shot-putter). Power requires both force and velocity.

items.q: What does the 'I' in FITT stand for?
items.why: Students guess. FITT = Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type - the variables you adjust to apply progressive overload. Intensity = how hard (e.g., % of 1RM or heart rate).
Correct answer: 1999. “1894” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.correct: Transparency, accountability and rule of law
items.why: Correct answer: Transparency, accountability and rule of law. “Centralised decision-making only” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If FIFA has 211 member associations and a 2/3 supermajority is needed for amendments, votes required is?
items.why: The real figure is 141. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
